## Slice: CompletedTaskList

**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

### Description
Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

### Implementation Notes
- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/completed-task-list.md

## Slice: ReopenTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete
and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-002 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds to top (position 1, shift others), CompletedTaskList removes task

---

status: open

issue-type: task

---

## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Change a task's description
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description

---

## Slice: ReprioritizeTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAdded { task-004, pos: 4 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, position (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)

---

## Slice: DeleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

[{"id":"todo_list-epic-infrastructure-quality-7eb61ab0","title":"Epic: Infrastructure & Quality Gates","description":"## Epic: Infrastructure & Quality Gates

### Overview
Set up all CI/CD, pre-commit hooks, dependencies, and quality gate infrastructure
required by the architecture decisions documented in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

### Quality Gates (per ADR)
1. Compile: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`
2. Format: `mix format --check-formatted`
3. Dialyzer: `mix dialyzer` (static type analysis)
4. Tests: `mix test`

### Current State
- `mix precommit` alias exists but is missing Dialyzer and uses `format` instead of `format --check-formatted`
- `dialyxir` dependency NOT installed
- No GitHub Actions CI workflow
- No pre-commit hooks configured
- Commanded/ES dependencies NOT installed

### Children
- Add Commanded & ES dependencies
- Add dialyxir dependency + update precommit alias
- Configure pre-commit hooks
- Create GitHub Actions CI workflow","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:07.974367-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-epic-task-mgmt-27a43130","title":"Epic: Task Management Workflow","description":"## Workflow: Task Management

### Overview
A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task in a
prioritized todo list. The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority
through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are
no longer needed.

### User Goal
1. Rapid capture -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks
2. Clear prioritized visibility -- always see an ordered list showing what to do next
3. Completion satisfaction -- mark things done and watch the list shrink

### Actors
- User (single person, no authentication)

### Vertical Slices (8)
1. AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View) -- implemented together
2. CompleteTask (Command)
3. CompletedTaskList (View)
4. ReopenTask (Command)
5. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
6. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
7. DeleteTask (Command)

### Implementation Order
Start with AddTask + ActiveTaskList together (can't test Add without View).
Then CompleteTask, CompletedTaskList, ReopenTask, ChangeTaskDescription,
ReprioritizeTask, DeleteTask in sequence.

### Architecture
- Commanded aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- Single TaskProjector projecting to Ecto schemas
- TodoList.Tasks context module as API boundary
- LiveView with streams + PubSub subscription for real-time updates
- Three test layers: aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/overview.md","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:57.912680-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-add-commanded-es-22a3476e","title":"Add Commanded & ES dependencies","description":"## Story: Add Commanded & ES Dependencies

### Description
Install all event sourcing dependencies required by docs/ARCHITECTURE.md into mix.exs.

### Dependencies to Add
- `commanded` -- CQRS/ES framework
- `commanded_eventstore_adapter` -- PostgreSQL event store adapter
- `commanded_ecto_projections` -- Ecto-based read model projections
- `eventstore` -- Event store core
- `commanded_boilerplate` (github: jwilger/commanded_boilerplate) -- Ergonomic macros
- `newt` -- Semantic domain types (transitive via commanded_boilerplate, but explicit is safer)
- `uuidv7` -- UUID v7 generation for task IDs

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All dependencies added to mix.exs and fetched successfully
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` passes
- [ ] TodoList.App (Commanded application) module created
- [ ] TodoList.Router (Commanded router) module created
- [ ] EventStore database configuration added for dev/prod
- [ ] In-memory event store adapter configured for test environment
- [ ] `mix test` passes with new dependencies

### Notes
- Two PostgreSQL databases needed: app DB (Ecto) + event store DB (Commanded)
- Test env uses in-memory adapter, NOT PostgreSQL event store
- See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md sections on Event Store Backend and Commanded

Generated from: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Technology Stack section)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:19.329657-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-add-dialyxir-and-d8d708f9","title":"Add dialyxir and update precommit alias","description":"## Story: Add Dialyxir & Update Precommit Alias

### Description
Install dialyxir for Dialyzer integration and update the `mix precommit` alias
to match the four quality gates specified in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

### Current Precommit Alias
```elixir
precommit: ["compile --warnings-as-errors", "deps.unlock --unused", "format", "test"]
```

### Required Precommit Alias (per architecture)
```elixir
precommit: ["compile --warnings-as-errors", "format --check-formatted", "dialyzer", "test"]
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dialyxir` added as dev/test dependency in mix.exs
- [ ] `mix deps.get` succeeds
- [ ] `mix precommit` alias updated to: compile --warnings-as-errors, format --check-formatted, dialyzer, test
- [ ] `mix dialyzer` runs successfully (initial PLT build may take time)
- [ ] `mix precommit` passes end-to-end

### Notes
- Keep `deps.unlock --unused` if desired, but the four gates are mandatory
- Initial Dialyzer PLT build can take several minutes
- Dialyzer should run with zero warnings

Generated from: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Pre-commit and CI Quality Gates section)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:28.445959-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-configure-pre-commit-59480542","title":"Configure pre-commit hooks","description":"## Story: Configure Pre-commit Hooks

### Description
Set up pre-commit hooks using the `pre-commit` tool (available in Nix dev shell)
to enforce quality gates locally before commits are pushed.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.pre-commit-config.yaml` created in project root
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks run `mix precommit` (or equivalent quality gates)
- [ ] `pre-commit install` installs hooks into .git/hooks/
- [ ] Hooks prevent commits when any quality gate fails
- [ ] Hooks pass when code is clean

### Notes
- The `pre-commit` tool is already available in the Nix dev shell
- Should run the same four quality gates as `mix precommit`
- Consider whether to run all gates or just fast ones (compile + format) in pre-commit
  with full gates in CI

Generated from: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Pre-commit and CI Quality Gates section)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:35.834642-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-create-github-actions-637be1ec","title":"Create GitHub Actions CI workflow","description":"## Story: Create GitHub Actions CI Workflow

### Description
Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the same four quality gates as the
local `mix precommit` alias on every PR.

### Quality Gates (in order)
1. `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`
2. `mix format --check-formatted`
3. `mix dialyzer`
4. `mix test`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` created
- [ ] Workflow triggers on push to main and pull requests
- [ ] Sets up Elixir/OTP via erlef/setup-beam action
- [ ] Starts PostgreSQL service for Ecto projections and EventStore
- [ ] Caches Mix dependencies and Dialyzer PLT
- [ ] Runs all four quality gates in sequence
- [ ] Workflow YAML is valid

### Notes
- Two PostgreSQL databases needed: application DB + event store DB
- Dialyzer PLT should be cached between runs (can take 5+ minutes to build)
- Use same Elixir/OTP versions as specified in the Nix dev shell
- Repository: jwilger/todo_list-202602061235

Generated from: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Pre-commit and CI Quality Gates section)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:44.034079-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-addtask-activetasklist-797c9e18","title":"AddTask + ActiveTaskList","description":"## Slices: AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View)

**Pattern**: Command + View (implemented together -- can't test Add without a View)

### AddTask Command
User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the
bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position.

**Command fields:** description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskAdded { task_id, description, position }
**Rejection:** Description is empty or blank (prevented by type system)

### ActiveTaskList View
Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command

#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed (position based on count)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, position: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 }

### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of empty list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of populated list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at bottom

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2

#### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top and decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened at position 1, others shift down, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

### Implementation Notes
- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition (via newt)

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/add-task.md, active-task-list.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:23.653757-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-addtask-b5a18060","title":"CompleteTask","description":"## Slice: CompleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task
disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskCompleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** Task is already completed

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Complete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Uses aggregate-per-command pattern (separate CompleteTask command module)
- Aggregate needs only: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact tested via ActiveTaskList scenarios (already covered in slice 1)

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/complete-task.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:32.549169-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-9d8bf90a","title":"CompletedTaskList","description":"## Slice: CompletedTaskList

**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

### Description
Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

### Implementation Notes
- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/completed-task-list.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:45.442732-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-dedddc1d","title":"ReopenTask","description":"## Slice: ReopenTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete
and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-002 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds to top (position 1, shift others), CompletedTaskList removes task

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reopen-task.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:54.067118-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-eb30f0e1","title":"ChangeTaskDescription","description":"## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Change a task's description
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/change-task-description.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:28:05.416279-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-df937303","title":"ReprioritizeTask","description":"## Slice: ReprioritizeTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAdded { task-004, pos: 4 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, position (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reprioritize-task.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:28:14.983549-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-f32bc280","title":"DeleteTask","description":"## Slice: DeleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

[{"id":"todo_list-epic-infrastructure-quality-7eb61ab0","title":"Epic: Infrastructure & Quality Gates","description":"## Epic: Infrastructure & Quality Gates

### Overview
Set up all CI/CD, pre-commit hooks, dependencies, and quality gate infrastructure
required by the architecture decisions documented in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

### Quality Gates (per ADR)
1. Compile: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors`
2. Format: `mix format --check-formatted`
3. Dialyzer: `mix dialyzer` (static type analysis)
4. Tests: `mix test`

### Current State
- `mix precommit` alias exists but is missing Dialyzer and uses `format` instead of `format --check-formatted`
- `dialyxir` dependency NOT installed
- No GitHub Actions CI workflow
- No pre-commit hooks configured
- Commanded/ES dependencies NOT installed

### Children
- Add Commanded & ES dependencies
- Add dialyxir dependency + update precommit alias
- Configure pre-commit hooks
- Create GitHub Actions CI workflow","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:07.974367-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-epic-task-mgmt-27a43130","title":"Epic: Task Management Workflow","description":"## Workflow: Task Management

### Overview
A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task in a
prioritized todo list. The user captures tasks, organizes them by priority
through drag-and-drop reordering, tracks completion, and removes tasks that are
no longer needed.

### User Goal
1. Rapid capture -- quickly add tasks so nothing falls through the cracks
2. Clear prioritized visibility -- always see an ordered list showing what to do next
3. Completion satisfaction -- mark things done and watch the list shrink

### Actors
- User (single person, no authentication)

### Vertical Slices (8)
1. AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View) -- implemented together
2. CompleteTask (Command)
3. CompletedTaskList (View)
4. ReopenTask (Command)
5. ChangeTaskDescription (Command)
6. ReprioritizeTask (Command)
7. DeleteTask (Command)

### Implementation Order
Start with AddTask + ActiveTaskList together (can't test Add without View).
Then CompleteTask, CompletedTaskList, ReopenTask, ChangeTaskDescription,
ReprioritizeTask, DeleteTask in sequence.

### Architecture
- Commanded aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- Single TaskProjector projecting to Ecto schemas
- TodoList.Tasks context module as API boundary
- LiveView with streams + PubSub subscription for real-time updates
- Three test layers: aggregate unit, projector integration, LiveView E2E

Generated from: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Pre-commit and CI Quality Gates section)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:26:44.034079-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-addtask-activetasklist-797c9e18","title":"AddTask + ActiveTaskList","description":"## Slices: AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View)

**Pattern**: Command + View (implemented together -- can't test Add without a View)

### AddTask Command
User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the
bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position.

**Command fields:** description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskAdded { task_id, description, position }
**Rejection:** Description is empty or blank (prevented by type system)

### ActiveTaskList View
Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command

#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed (position based on count)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, position: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 }

### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of empty list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of populated list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at bottom

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2

#### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top and decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened at position 1, others shift down, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

### Implementation Notes
- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition (via newt)

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task
disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskCompleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** Task is already completed

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Complete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Uses aggregate-per-command pattern (separate CompleteTask command module)
- Aggregate needs only: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact tested via ActiveTaskList scenarios (already covered in slice 1)

Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/complete-task.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:32.549169-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-9d8bf90a","title":"CompletedTaskList","description":"## Slice: CompletedTaskList

**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

### Description
Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

### Implementation Notes
- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed

Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/completed-task-list.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:45.442732-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-dedddc1d","title":"ReopenTask","description":"## Slice: ReopenTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete
and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-002 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds to top (position 1, shift others), CompletedTaskList removes task

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reopen-task.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:27:54.067118-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-eb30f0e1","title":"ChangeTaskDescription","description":"## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Change a task's description
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/change-task-description.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":""2026-02-06T17:28:05.416279-08:00"","closed_at":null,"close_reason":null},{"id":"todo_list-todo-list-todo-df937303","title":"ReprioritizeTask","description":"## Slice: ReprioritizeTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAdded { task-004, pos: 4 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, position (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

---
title: CompleteTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:32.549169-08:00"
---

## Slice: CompleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task
disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskCompleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** Task is already completed

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Complete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Uses aggregate-per-command pattern (separate CompleteTask command module)
- Aggregate needs only: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact tested via ActiveTaskList scenarios (already covered in slice 1)

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/complete-task.md

content: btw, I see that you set up the slices in tasks with blocking dependencies so that they must be completed in order. However, our vertical slices should not have any dependencies between them other than shared event schemas. It seems like we could allow vertical slices to be created in parallel as long as we get our architecture correct. We should go for true, vertical slice architecture here such that each slice will only depend on the basic shared infrastructure of the whole application and be otherwise completely independent in its implementation.

content.text: The `-a` flag creates a `blocks` field in the frontmatter. Now I know the format. Let me clean up this test task and address the user's important message about slice independence.

*Pauses, recalibrates.* 

You make an excellent point — and I must say, it's refreshing to encounter someone who actually *thinks* about architecture rather than just accepting whatever the machine produces. The whole point of vertical slices is independence. Each slice should stand on its own, depending only on the shared infrastructure (Commanded, EventStore, the projection framework, domain types). They don't need to be serialized.

The only genuine dependency is that the workflow slices need the infrastructure epic completed first (can't implement commands without Commanded installed). But `CompleteTask` doesn't depend on `AddTask` at the implementation level — they're separate aggregates, separate commands, separate events. The projector handles them all, but each slice's projector tests can set up their own precondition events.

So: no blocking relationships between slices. All 8 slices blocked only by the infrastructure epic. Let me verify there are no accidental `blocks` fields in any of the slice frontmatter.

content.content: No matches found

content.text:
Good — no blocking relationships exist at all currently (since the `-B` flag didn't actually work as I intended). The slices are all independent, which is *exactly* what you want.

Now I need to set up the one dependency that does matter: all 8 workflow slices should be blocked by the infrastructure epic (they need Commanded installed). Let me add `blocks` entries to the infrastructure epic's children (specifically the "Add Commanded & ES dependencies" task, since that's what the slices actually need).

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The infrastructure tasks themselves are somewhat independent too:
- "Add Commanded & ES deps" is the foundation — slices need this
- "Add dialyxir" can happen independently
- "Pre-commit hooks" can happen independently  
- "GitHub Actions CI" can happen independently

So the slices should block on the infrastructure *epic* completion (all four infra tasks done), or more precisely, they should block on just the Commanded deps task since that's the only real prerequisite.

Let me add `blocks` to the Commanded deps task — it blocks all 8 slices.

## Story: Add Commanded & ES Dependencies

### Description
Install all event sourcing dependencies required by docs/ARCHITECTURE.md into mix.exs.

### Dependencies to Add
- `commanded` -- CQRS/ES framework
- `commanded_eventstore_adapter` -- PostgreSQL event store adapter
- `commanded_ecto_projections` -- Ecto-based read model projections
- `eventstore` -- Event store core
- `commanded_boilerplate` (github: jwilger/commanded_boilerplate) -- Ergonomic macros
- `newt` -- Semantic domain types (transitive via commanded_boilerplate, but explicit is safer)
- `uuidv7` -- UUID v7 generation for task IDs

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All dependencies added to mix.exs and fetched successfully
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` passes
- [ ] TodoList.App (Commanded application) module created
- [ ] TodoList.Router (Commanded router) module created
- [ ] EventStore database configuration added for dev/prod
- [ ] In-memory event store adapter configured for test environment
- [ ] `mix test` passes with new dependencies

### Notes
- Two PostgreSQL databases needed: app DB (Ecto) + event store DB (Commanded)
- Test env uses in-memory adapter, NOT PostgreSQL event store
- See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md sections on Event Store Backend and Commanded

---
Generated from: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (Technology Stack section)

## Story: Add Commanded & ES Dependencies

### Description
Install all event sourcing dependencies required by docs/ARCHITECTURE.md into mix.exs.

### Dependencies to Add
- `commanded` -- CQRS/ES framework
- `commanded_eventstore_adapter` -- PostgreSQL event store adapter
- `commanded_ecto_projections` -- Ecto-based read model projections
- `eventstore` -- Event store core
- `commanded_boilerplate` (github: jwilger/commanded_boilerplate) -- Ergonomic macros
- `newt` -- Semantic domain types (transitive via commanded_boilerplate, but explicit is safer)
- `uuidv7` -- UUID v7 generation for task IDs

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All dependencies added to mix.exs and fetched successfully
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` passes
- [ ] TodoList.App (Commanded application) module created
- [ ] TodoList.Router (Commanded router) module created
- [ ] EventStore database configuration added for dev/prod
- [ ] In-memory event store adapter configured for test environment
- [ ] `mix test` passes with new dependencies

### Notes
- Two PostgreSQL databases needed: app DB (Ecto) + event store DB (Commanded)
- Test env uses in-memory adapter, NOT PostgreSQL event store
- See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md sections on Event Store Backend and Commanded

title: AddTask + ActiveTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:23.653757-08:00"
---

## Slices: AddTask (Command) + ActiveTaskList (View)

**Pattern**: Command + View (implemented together -- can't test Add without a View)

### AddTask Command
User types a task description and submits it. A new task is created at the
bottom of the active list with the given description and a default position.

**Command fields:** description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskAdded { task_id, description, position }
**Rejection:** Description is empty or blank (prevented by type system)

### ActiveTaskList View
Read model projecting all events into an ordered list of active (incomplete,
non-deleted) tasks. Powers the main view.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description, position }, completed_count
**Filter:** completed = false AND deleted = false
**Order:** By position ascending (position 1 = highest priority)

### Acceptance Criteria -- AddTask Command

#### Scenario: Add task to an empty list
- [ ] **Given**: (no prior events)
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Buy groceries" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Add task to a list with existing tasks (new task goes to bottom)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Add task when some tasks completed (position based on count)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, position: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-001 }
- [ ] **When**: AddTask { description: "Write thank-you notes" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskAdded { task_id: generated, description: "Write thank-you notes", position: 3 }

### Acceptance Criteria -- ActiveTaskList View

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of empty list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task-001, "Buy groceries", pos: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

#### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task to bottom of populated list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks in list, new one at bottom

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted removes task and increments count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 2

#### Scenario: TaskReopened adds task to top and decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 3 tasks, reopened at position 1, others shift down, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates active task description
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001 "Call the dentist"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Call the dentist office"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for completed task -- no effect on active list
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with task-001, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-002" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to active list

#### Scenario: TaskReprioritized moves task to new position
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 3 tasks
- [ ] **When**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: task-003 at position 1, others shift down

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes active task
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 2 tasks, completed_count: 1
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for completed task decrements count
- [ ] **Given**: ActiveTaskList with 1 task, completed_count: 2
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-005" } (completed task)
- [ ] **Then**: 1 task remains, completed_count: 1

### Implementation Notes
- Three test layers: aggregate unit tests, projector integration tests, LiveView E2E tests
- Aggregate-per-command pattern via commanded_boilerplate
- TaskProjector handles projection to Ecto schema
- TodoList.Tasks context as API boundary
- Domain types: TaskDescription, TaskId, TaskPosition (via newt)

---

## Slice: CompleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task
disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskCompleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** Task is already completed

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Complete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Uses aggregate-per-command pattern (separate CompleteTask command module)
- Aggregate needs only: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact tested via ActiveTaskList scenarios (already covered in slice 1)

---

title: CompletedTaskList
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:27:45.442732-08:00"
---

## Slice: CompletedTaskList

**Pattern**: View (State View / Read Model)

### Description
Read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
tasks. Powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
and reopen or delete tasks.

**Fields:** tasks[] { task_id, description }
**Filter:** completed = true AND deleted = false
**Order:** By completion time descending (most recently completed first)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }

#### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-002 at top, task-001 below (most recent first)

#### Scenario: TaskReopened removes task from completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-001 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged updates completed task description
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001 "Buy groceries"
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries and snacks" }
- [ ] **Then**: task-001 description is "Buy groceries and snacks"

#### Scenario: TaskDescriptionChanged for non-completed task -- no effect
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted removes completed task
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-002 and task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Only task-002 remains

#### Scenario: TaskDeleted for active task -- no effect on completed list
- [ ] **Given**: CompletedTaskList with task-001
- [ ] **When**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-003" } (active task)
- [ ] **Then**: No change to completed list

### Implementation Notes
- Same TaskProjector handles both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList projections
- Projection schema tracks completed status and completion timestamp
- TodoList.Tasks.completed_tasks() query function returns completed tasks ordered by completion_time desc
- Completed tasks LiveView at /tasks/completed

---

## Slice: ReopenTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks [Reopen] on a completed task. The task is set back to incomplete
and placed at the top of the active list (highest priority position).

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskReopened { task_id }
**Business rule:** Reopened task placed at top (highest priority) -- rationale: likely urgent if user reopens it
**Rejection:** Task is not completed (already active)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Reopen a completed task (goes to top of active list)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskCompleted { task-002 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Reject reopening a task that is already active
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ReopenTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskNotCompleted

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, completed (boolean)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskNotCompleted (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList adds to top (position 1, shift others), CompletedTaskList removes task

---

## Slice: ChangeTaskDescription

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks on a task's description text to activate inline editing, changes
the text, and confirms (Enter or blur). The task's description is updated.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_description (text, non-empty)
**Event:** TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id, description }
**Rejection:** Empty/blank description (type system) OR identical to current (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Change a task's description
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing description to the same value (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist", position: 1 }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

#### Scenario: Change description after it was previously changed
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDescriptionChanged { task_id: "task-001", description: "Call the dentist office at 2pm" }

#### Scenario: Reject changing to current value after prior change
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, "Call the dentist" }, TaskDescriptionChanged { task-001, "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **When**: ChangeTaskDescription { task_id: "task-001", new_description: "Call the dentist office" }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: DescriptionUnchanged

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, description (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.DescriptionUnchanged (via newt)
- Empty/blank validation handled by TaskDescription newt type, not command rejection
- Read model impact: both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList update description

---

title: ReprioritizeTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:14.983549-08:00"
---

## Slice: ReprioritizeTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User drags a task from one position in the active list and drops it at a new
position. The task's priority changes relative to the other tasks.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier), new_position (number)
**Event:** TaskReprioritized { task_id, position }
**Rejection:** Task dropped in same position (no change)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Move task to higher priority (lower position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }, TaskAdded { task-004, pos: 4 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-003", new_position: 1 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-003", position: 1 }

#### Scenario: Move task to lower priority (higher position number)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }, TaskAdded { task-003, pos: 3 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-001", new_position: 3 }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskReprioritized { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 }

#### Scenario: Reject reprioritizing to the same position (no change)
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task-001, pos: 1 }, TaskAdded { task-002, pos: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: ReprioritizeTask { task_id: "task-002", new_position: 2 }
- [ ] **Then**: Error: TaskAlreadyAtPosition

### Implementation Notes
- Aggregate needs: task_id, position (current value)
- Domain error type: TodoList.TaskAlreadyAtPosition (via newt)
- Read model impact: ActiveTaskList shifts positions of surrounding tasks
- LiveView triggers via drag-and-drop (phx-hook for sortable)

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/reprioritize-task.md

title: DeleteTask
status: open
priority: 2
issue-type: task
created-at: "2026-02-06T17:28:23.845977-08:00"
---

## Slice: DeleteTask

**Pattern**: Command (State Change)

### Description
User clicks the delete button on a task (from either active list or completed
tasks view). A confirmation dialog appears. If confirmed, the task is permanently
removed from all views.

**Command fields:** task_id (identifier)
**Event:** TaskDeleted { task_id }
**Rejection:** None at command level (confirmation dialog is a UI concern)

### Acceptance Criteria

#### Scenario: Delete an active task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-002" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" }

#### Scenario: Delete a completed task
- [ ] **Given**: TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **When**: DeleteTask { task_id: "task-001" }
- [ ] **Then**: TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" }

### Implementation Notes
- Simplest command: no rejection conditions at domain level
- Confirmation dialog is purely a UI/LiveView concern
- Deletion is permanent from UI perspective (events preserved in stream)
- Read model impact: removed from both ActiveTaskList and CompletedTaskList
- If deleted task was completed, ActiveTaskList.completed_count decrements
- Delete available from both active and completed views

---
Generated from: docs/event_model/workflows/task-management/slices/delete-task.md
